claims.text: APM supports five package layouts: .apm/, SKILL.md, skills/<name>/SKILL.md, hooks/*.json, and plugin.json.
Primitives under .apm/ are hoisted individually into the consumer's runtime directories during install.
When SKILL.md is present at package root, the entire directory is installed as a unit to <target>/skills/<name>/.
In HYBRID packages, apm.yml.description and SKILL.md description are independent fields that APM never merges.
APM copies SKILL.md byte-for-byte into <target>/skills/<name>/ and never reads or mutates the description field.
allowed-tools lives exclusively in SKILL.md frontmatter and is consumed by the agent runtime.
In skill collections, each skills/<name>/SKILL.md directory is promoted to <target>/skills/<name>/ during install.
apm install --skill flag persists selected skills to apm.yml as a skills: field and to apm.lock.yaml as skill_subset.
apm install --skill '*' resets the selection to install all skills in a collection.
Skill collection frontmatter name field must match the directory name if present.
All frontmatter values in skill collections must be ASCII-only.
Hook packages with hooks/*.json files deploy each hook to the target's hooks runtime directory.
Hook packages without apm.yml, SKILL.md, or plugin.json treat the directory as a hook bundle.
Plugin collection layout dissects plugin artifacts and maps them via _map_plugin_artifacts.
For skill collections, if apm.yml is absent, APM synthesizes minimal metadata from the directory name.
